entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: An important cereal crop grown in hot and dry agroecologies with diverse uses as food, feed, fodder, fuel, fiber, and industrial crop

entities.description: Environmental conditions where sorghum is primarily grown

entities.description: Country where sorghum is grown during rainy and post-rainy seasons

entities.name: RAINY AND POST-RAINY SEASONS
entities.description: Specific seasons for sorghum cultivation in India

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, which develops sorghum parental lines, hybrids, and varieties

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Continent where sorghum is grown under diverse ecologies and where ICRISAT provides research support

entities.name: KAOLIANG
entities.description: A type of sorghum from China that offers tolerance to cold stress

entities.name: CHINA
entities.description: Country where Kaoliang is grown and research on biotic and abiotic stresses is conducted

entities.name: COLD STRESS

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: General stresses that are the focus of sorghum research programs in China

entities.name: STRIGA
entities.description: A parasitic organism that sorghum varieties in Africa are developed to be tolerant to

entities.name: INTSORMIL
entities.description: International Sorghum and Millet organization focusing on improving production in developing countries

entities.name: ACID SOIL
entities.description: A soil condition for which resistant sorghum genotypes were developed

entities.name: LATIN AMERICA
entities.description: Region where acid soil-resistant sorghum genotypes are grown

entities.name: AUSTRALIA
entities.description: Country focusing on developing sorghum cultivars tolerant to midge and drought

entities.name: SORGHUM MIDGE
entities.description: A pest that Australian sorghum cultivars are developed to tolerate

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: An environmental stress that Australian sorghum cultivars are developed to tolerate

entities.name: UNITED STATES
entities.description: Country where sorghum is grown in 14 states for feed, fodder, and fuel

entities.name: US DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Organization that funds sorghum research for genetic enhancement

entities.name: SORGHUM CHECKOFF
entities.description: Fund that supports sorghum research for genetic enhancement

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The process of improving sorghum genetics for specific uses like feed, fodder, and fuel

entities.name: EUROPE
entities.description: Continent where sorghum is cultivated for cattle feed

entities.name: CATTLE FEED
entities.description: A specific use of sorghum cultivation in Europe

entities.name: BROOMCORN
entities.description: A type of sorghum used for industrial purposes

entities.name: PAPER MAKING
entities.description: An industrial use for broomcorn sorghum
